benchmark_scope: Prove the Lonely Runner Conjecture for n=11 runners: given 10 runners with distinct positive integer speeds on the unit circle, there exists a time when all are simultaneously at distance >= 1/11 from the fixed runner.
planning_constraints: Do not cite the Barajas-Serra (2008) result that LRC holds for n <= 7 as a given fact; if that result is needed, its proof must be derived from the seeds provided.
Do not use the Three-Distance Theorem (Steinhaus conjecture) as a black box; any application of it must be proved from the seed definitions.
The naive union bound gives total measure 20/11 > 1, which is insufficient; an argument exploiting the arithmetic structure of the speeds is required.
Do not directly generalize a proof for n < 11 to n = 11 without a complete argument for each generalization step.
If a Fourier / spectral method is proposed, the key estimates must be derived, not assumed.
Do not treat the conjecture itself or any equivalent reformulation as an available intermediate fact.
